item.title: An unprecedented data center boom means new challenges for Texas. Find out what’s planned near you.
item.description: A Texas Tribune analysis identified at least 248 planned data center projects. Opponents fear the projects will spike Texans’ electric bills and make the grid less reliable. But industry representatives say they promise huge economic gains.
item.dc:creator: Emily Foxhall

item.title: Local Texas election officials await appointment of new secretary of state as midterm preparations ramp up
item.dc:creator: Natalia Contreras

item.title: Where are the data centers in Texas? Help us continue our reporting.
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Balcones Canyonlands wildlife refuge, home to endangered songbird, adds more than 600 acres
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Second screwworm case in Texas confirmed as Abbott expands state disaster declaration
item.dc:creator: Stephen Simpson

item.title: Texas county rescinds its data center moratorium after $100 million lawsuit from developer
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Eight Senate Republicans broke ranks this week. John Cornyn, spurned by Trump in his primary, didn’t.
item.description: The Texas senator’s defeat to Ken Paxton sparked talk he’d join the “YOLO Caucus” of Republicans defecting from Donald Trump. This week, he sided with Senate GOP leadership on every key vote.
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Parkinson’s disease is consistently linked to pesticide exposure, putting Rio Grande Valley farmworkers at risk
item.description: RGV researchers have been struck by how many people had Parkinson’s disease, a neurological condition that has been linked to pesticides and other environmental toxins.
item.dc:creator: Public Health Watch

item.title: Christian Menefee’s congressional win is a generational shift backed by cryptocurrency
item.description: Menefee, 38, unseated U.S. Rep. Al Green, 78, to represent a redrawn Harris County congressional district. The men faced off after Republicans combined parts of their current districts into one.
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Elected Officials Directory
item.description: To help Texans know more about their representatives, we’ve gathered the bios, contact information and more for the occupants of the state’s highest offices.

item.title: Government Salaries Explorer
item.description: Review the compensation of hundreds of thousands of state and municipal employees in Texas.

item.title: Texas Public Schools Explorer
item.description: Review enrollment trends, demographics, teacher experience, pay and outcomes for all schools, and more.

item.title: What to know about screwworm in Texas
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Detained Guatemalan woman who required urgent ovarian surgery for months released by ICE
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Texas lawmakers put limits on cities’ abilities to enact progressive policies. Some want to go further.
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Advocates ask court for chance to defend Texas Dream Act, in-state tuition for undocumented students
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: Texas now requires cities to do an audit before raising property taxes. Some small towns can’t afford it.
item.dc:creator: by

item.title: First U.S. screwworm case confirmed in South Texas
item.dc:creator: Berenice Garcia

item.title: National Democrats aim to flip 12 Texas House seats under newly expanded target list

item.title: Corpus Christi delays decision on nearly billion-dollar water plant despite impending crisis

item.title: Sid Miller to join Democratic nominee for agriculture commissioner at data center forum

item.title: Texas lifts commercial drivers license ban for temporary agricultural workers
